Here, within the cool glass of The Jar, another summer wanes, the miniature sun casting long shadows across a landscape slowly, relentlessly changing. Though vibrant flora still carpets the soil, a subtle unease permeates the scene, marked by the recent, desperate acts of the sleek, obsidian Vorren—a predator driven to hunt even as its own numbers dwindle. The ghost of the Thornback, a creature lost to time within this small world, serves as a poignant reminder that even in a contained paradise, nothing truly lasts, and one wonders what fragile balance will hold when winter arrives.

Here, within the cool embrace of sculpted glass, the miniature world persists into its first summer, a delicate bloom of emerald life clinging to existence. The swift, scaled Kreel, once numerous, now enact a grim ballet of survival, their numbers thinning with each desperate lunge – a stark reminder that even in paradise, shadows fall. But the watchful Vorren, sleek and cunning, moves amongst them, and one wonders if this fragile balance will hold as the seasons turn within The Jar.
